title: Daily Institutional Market Research Dashboard
as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh

market_read.headline: Hot PPI Extends the Inflation Shock While AI Infrastructure Still Bids
market_read.summary: The May 13 tape is event-driven and more fragile than the futures headline suggests. April PPI confirmed that the prior CPI shock was not isolated, which keeps Fed cuts, Treasury supply, oil, and duration multiples under pressure. The offset is still equity dispersion: AI infrastructure, China-semiconductor optionality, and nearby earnings/IPO catalysts are drawing capital, but those setups now need to work against a higher-rate, higher-input-cost backdrop.
key_points: April PPI was reported at +0.7% month over month and +4.9% year over year, with core wholesale inflation also firm; the prior dashboard's pending-PPI risk is now a confirmed macro negative.
April CPI from May 12 was already hot at +0.6% month over month and +3.8% year over year, so the two-day inflation impulse makes the Fed's 3.50%-3.75% hold harder to fade.

Futures were still firm before the open, with AI/chip names helped by China-trip optionality, analyst target hikes, and memory/optical/storage demand, but this is dispersion under macro pressure rather than clean risk-on.
Oil remains a transmission risk: Reuters reported sharp global inventory draws while Iran-related supply risk keeps crude above the comfort zone for inflation-sensitive investors.

Closest catalysts: Cisco after the close today, Treasury 30-year supply today, Applied Materials tomorrow, Walmart/Deere consumer and industrial reads tomorrow, and Cerebras/Fervo IPO aftermarket demand.

Live direct quote capture remains incomplete. The dashboard uses source-reported moves and labels screen-only movers as lower confidence. Treat all opportunity cases as scenario analysis, not personalized financial advice.
what_changed_since_prior_snapshot: PPI moved from pending/stale to confirmed hot inflation data using AP and MarketWatch reporting; official BLS PPI schedule is linked, but the current BLS release did not surface reliably in the crawl.
Macro stance was tightened: the dashboard now emphasizes Fed-cut repricing, Treasury supply, and oil pass-through as the main constraints on equity multiple expansion.
AI/chip section was kept but reframed around dispersion and event risk rather than broad beta; Cisco and AMAT are highlighted as the next tests.
Mover section adds source-conflict flags for screen-only gainers and keeps low-quality chatter out of the thesis stack.
market_read.research_frame: Investment research and scenario analysis for learning and decision support only; not personalized financial advice.

signals.title: PPI confirms the inflation problem is broader than CPI
signals.summary: April wholesale prices were reported up 0.7% month over month and 4.9% year over year. That makes the May 12 CPI shock harder to dismiss and increases the burden on soft retail/labor data before investors can price an easier Fed path.

signals.summary: The Fed's current 3.50%-3.75% target range already reflected elevated inflation risk. Hot CPI plus hot PPI raises the bar for cuts and pressures long-duration equities, small caps, unprofitable growth, and housing-linked cyclicals.

signals.title: AI infrastructure remains the clearest equity offset
signals.summary: Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, Micron, Intel, Qualcomm, optical, storage, and server names are still receiving demand and analyst-support signals. The higher-quality interpretation is not broad risk appetite; it is a specific AI infrastructure revision cycle competing with macro-rate headwinds.

opportunities.name: AI infrastructure breadth under rate stress
opportunities.setup: AI capex is broadening from GPUs into HBM, optical interconnects, networking, storage, and data-center materials while rates pressure generic growth exposure.
opportunities.mispricing: The market may still be underweighting second-order bottleneck suppliers if investors focus too narrowly on Nvidia. The offset is that higher PPI can compress multiples before estimates rise.
bull_case: Tight memory and optical supply improves pricing power
Cisco/AMAT confirm AI orders and capex breadth
bear_case: Hot inflation lifts discount rates and compresses AI multiples
AI capex expectations are already crowded
disconfirming_evidence: Nvidia guide fails to clear elevated expectations

opportunities.name: Cisco as the cheapest near-term AI networking read-through
opportunities.setup: Cisco reports after the close and can validate or reject the idea that AI networking demand is spreading beyond the most expensive winners.
opportunities.mispricing: If investors view Cisco as a slow-growth legacy hardware name, clean AI-order commentary could have a higher surprise value than similar commentary from already-crowded AI leaders.
catalysts: Q3 FY26 results after May 13 close
bull_case: Supply constraints ease without margin damage
bear_case: AI backlog is too small relative to total revenue
disconfirming_evidence: Peers do not move positively after the print

opportunities.name: Fervo geothermal IPO as AI-power scarcity proxy
opportunities.setup: Fervo upsized its IPO as investors look for dependable power supply tied to data-center demand and clean-energy baseload narratives.
opportunities.mispricing: Public markets may pay a scarcity premium for scalable geothermal exposure if AI-load growth makes power availability a binding constraint. The risk is that project execution, financing cost, and immature public comps deserve a large discount.
bull_case: Data-center power demand expands the TAM
Geothermal baseload differentiates from intermittent renewables
bear_case: Capital intensity and execution risk dominate
disconfirming_evidence: Reduced pricing or low allocation quality

opportunities.name: Cerebras IPO: differentiated AI chip exposure with concentration risk
opportunities.setup: Cerebras reportedly raised its IPO range, putting a non-Nvidia AI-chip architecture in front of public investors during a strong AI infrastructure tape.
opportunities.mispricing: The upside case is that public investors value alternative AI compute supply more aggressively than private marks. The downside case is that customer concentration, Nvidia competition, and uncertain gross-margin durability demand a discount.
bull_case: AI compute demand is supply constrained
bear_case: Revenue concentration and customer quality concerns
Valuation leaves little room for execution errors
disconfirming_evidence: Post-IPO research focuses on concentration risk
No evidence of broad customer diversification

opportunities.name: AEP and grid capex: dilution today, load-growth thesis later
opportunities.setup: AEP's $2.6 billion equity offering pressured the stock but reinforces how much capital utilities need for grid and data-center load growth.
opportunities.mispricing: Near-term dilution can obscure the strategic value of regulated load growth, especially if AI/data-center demand makes grid investment visible. The risk is that higher rates make utility equity financing more expensive and lower allowed-return appeal.
bull_case: Power equipment suppliers benefit without utility dilution
disconfirming_evidence: Data-center load growth slows or shifts regions

opportunities.mispricing: The market may be rapidly repricing hidden asset value, but without deal economics, debt/cash-flow reconciliation, and sustainable core performance, the move can be mostly technical.

disconfirming_evidence: Share price gives back most of the move after volume normalizes

prices.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
prices.note: Direct live quote capture was unavailable in this environment. Price and move fields use source-reported premarket/live-news/mover-screen values and are labeled when not independently verified.

news.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
news.note: Fresh pass covered macro, Fed, policy, AI/chips, movers, earnings, IPOs, utilities/power, oil, and commentary/analyst-action sources.

macro_calendar.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
macro_calendar.note: PPI was updated from pending to hot using AP and MarketWatch reports. The BLS schedule page is linked, but the current BLS PPI release did not surface reliably in the crawl. CPI and FOMC are official primary sources.

filings.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
filings.note: Cerebras primary S-1 was used for risk context; revised IPO terms are from WSJ. Fervo terms are from the company press release. Deeper SEC review for all movers was not completed.

commentary_mosaic.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
commentary_mosaic.note: Included reported analyst target hikes, options-implied move commentary, financial press, and IPO reception signals. Reddit/social chatter was excluded because no corroborated high-quality signal was found in the scan window.

source_conflicts.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
source_conflicts.note: BLS PPI current-release crawl was unreliable versus AP/MarketWatch reports. Screen-only microcap movers are included only with low-confidence labels. IPO calendar pages can lag company/newswire revised terms.

theses.as_of: May 13, 2026 6:10 AM PT / 9:10 AM ET, post-PPI pre-open refresh
theses.note: All opportunity cases include bull/bear cases and disconfirming evidence and should be read as research scenarios, not personalized buy/sell advice.

failed_sources: Direct live-price/finance capture was unavailable or incomplete, so no real-time quote table was independently produced.
Official BLS current PPI release did not surface reliably during crawl; AP and MarketWatch are used for the post-release print while the BLS schedule is cited for release timing.
Some paywalled WSJ/Barron's-style items were available only through search-result excerpts or summarized accessible snippets; primary/company sources were preferred where possible.
